applied.note: New safeJsonLd() helper. Escapes <, >, & to \u003c/\u003e/\u0026 plus U+2028/U+2029 line separators. Regex authored with \u escape sequences (file is pure-ASCII) to avoid 'unterminated regex literal' from raw separator chars. Runtime-verified: </script> breakout neutralized.

Most critical (user-controlled bio/firstName/lastName/username). Replaced JSON.stringify(personSchema) and JSON.stringify(breadcrumbSchema) with safeJsonLd(). Added import { safeJsonLd } from '@/utils/jsonLd'.
organizationSchema + websiteSchema now use safeJsonLd(). Added import from '@/utils/jsonLd'.
courseSchema + breadcrumbSchema now use safeJsonLd(). Added import from '@utils/jsonLd' (matches file's existing @utils alias).
videoSchema now uses safeJsonLd(). Added import from '@utils/jsonLd'.

summary: Fixed FE-001 stored XSS across all 4 JSON-LD files (7 dangerouslySetInnerHTML blocks). Introduced safeJsonLd() helper that escapes <, >, & and U+2028/U+2029, preventing </script> breakout from user-controlled fields (notably bio on public profile). Did not touch the two non-JSON-LD dangerouslySetInnerHTML usages (referral/page.tsx and videos/[id]/playground/page.tsx — both CSS <style> blocks). 'cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit' passes with zero errors. Runtime test confirms <, >, & escaped and no raw </script> survives.
